zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Larkyn於1552年2月19日出生在英國肯特韋斯特 比可哈姆時，她的父親William Larkyn32歲，母親Joan Copland27歲。她於1575年2月9日與Alexander Betts在英國肯特明日香結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1596年11月7日在她的家鄉享年44歲，埋葬在英國肯特韋斯特 比可哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Larkyn于1552年2月19日出生在英国肯特韋斯特 比可哈姆时，她的父亲William Larkyn32岁，母亲Joan Copland27岁。她于1575年2月9日与Alexander Betts在英国肯特明日香结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1596年11月7日在她的家乡享年44岁，埋葬在英国肯特韋斯特 比可哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Larkyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from a medieval personal name, a diminutive of Lar-, Lor-, both pet forms of Lawrence , formed with the addition of the Middle English suffix -kin.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Lorcáin ‘descendant of Lorcán’, a personal name from a diminutive of lorc ‘fierce, cruel’. This Gaelic name was often regarded as an equivalent of English Lawrence .

variants.name: Lawrence
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Lorens, Laurence, from Latin Laurentius ‘man from Laurentum’, a place in Italy probably named from its laurels or bay trees. The name was borne by a Christian saint who was martyred at Rome in the 3rd century AD ; he enjoyed a considerable cult throughout Europe, with consequent popularity of the personal name (French Laurent, Italian, Spanish Lorenzo, Catalan Llorenç, Portuguese Lourenço, German Laurenz, Polish Wawrzyniec, etc.). In Britain this is a common name from the 12th century, with pet forms such as Law , Low , Lawrie , Laurie , Larry , Larkin , all of which are represented in surnames. There was also a feminine form Laurencia which may have given rise to the English surname. The surname is also borne by Jews among whom it is presumably an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Ashkenazic surnames. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. German Lorenz , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Slovenian Lavrenčič and Lovrenčič (patronymics from Lavrencij and Lovrenc, equivalents of Lawrence), Polish Wawrzyniak . Compare Larrance , Laurence , Lawerence , Lieurance , and Lowrance .

variants.name: Larkins
meanings: English: variant of Larkin 1, with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Larkyn was born on 19 February 1552, in West Peckham, Kent, England, her father, William Larkyn, was 32 and her mother, Joan Copland, was 27. She married Alexander Betts on 9 February 1575, in Langley, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 7 November 1596, in her hometown, at the age of 44, and was buried in West Peckham, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Larkyn est née le 19 février 1552, à West Peckham, Kent, Angleterre, son père, William Larkyn, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Joan Copland, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Alexander Betts le 9 février 1575, à Langley, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 7 novembre 1596, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterrée à West Peckham, Kent, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Larkynが1552年2月19日にイギリスケントウエスト ペックハムで生まれた時，父William Larkynは32歳，母Joan Coplandは27歳であった。1575年2月9日にイギリスケントラングリーでAlexander Bettsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1596年11月7日に故郷で44歳で死去し，イギリスケントウエスト ペックハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Larkyn(이)가 1552년 2월 19일 영국 켄트 웨스트 펙함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Larkyn(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Copland(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1575년 2월 9일 영국 켄트 랭글리에서 Alexander Betts(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1596년 11월 7일 그녀의 고향에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 웨스트 펙함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Larkyn nasceu em 19 de fevereiro de 1552, em West Peckham, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Larkyn, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Joan Copland, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Alexander Betts em 9 de fevereiro de 1575, em Langley, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Larkyn родилась 19 февраля 1552 г. в местности вест пеккхам, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени William Larkyn было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Copland, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alexander Betts 9 февраля 1575 г. в местности лангли, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 7 ноября 1596 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 44 лет и была похоронена в местности вест пеккхам, кэнт, Англия.
